MARETO HQ  —  NAVIGATION AND SIDEBAR GUIDE
Navigation and
Sidebar Guide
Quick Reference Guide — Mareto

What This Guide Covers

This guide explains how to move around Mareto — how the sidebar is organized, what each page does, and where to find the tools you need. If you are new to Mareto, start here.

The Sidebar: Your Main Navigation

The sidebar runs along the left side of every page in Mareto. It lists all the pages available to your account, each with an icon and label. The currently active page is highlighted in teal.

Important:

The pages in your sidebar depend on your organization’s configuration. Mareto supports different types of organizations — a shelter program will see different pages than a child and family services program. You will only see pages relevant to your work.

Active page shown with teal highlight in the sidebar

Opening and Closing the Sidebar

You can collapse the sidebar to give yourself a full-screen view of any page. This is useful when working in records, reviewing reports, or on smaller screens.

Closing the Sidebar

Click the X icon in the top-left corner, next to the Mareto logo.

Reopening the Sidebar

Click the hamburger icon (≡) that appears in the top-left corner of the page.

Sidebar Controls at a Glance

Namespace Selector

The dropdown below the logo shows your current workspace — for example, ‘HelpSeeker Demo 4’. If your organization operates multiple namespaces, use this to switch between them.

Namespace dropdown — click to switch between workspaces

Search Pages

The Search pages… bar at the top of the sidebar lets you jump to any page instantly by typing its name. Faster than scrolling, especially in larger configurations.

Type any page name to navigate directly

Expandable Pages (Sub-pages)

Some sidebar items have a dropdown arrow (∨). Clicking them expands to reveal sub-pages. For example, clicking Shelter reveals Shelter Operations, which in turn reveals Bed Check, Room Check, Shift Log, and Incident Reports.

Shelter expanded — click the arrow to reveal sub-pages

The Top Bar

The top bar appears on every page. It gives you access to notifications and your account.

Top bar: Menu, Notifications (bell), and Your account (initials)

ElementWhat it does
MenuAccess additional page or system options.
Bell iconView your notifications.
Your initials (e.g. KH)Opens your personal account menu.

Your Account Menu

Click your initials in the top-right corner to open the account menu.

Click your initials to open the account menu

OptionWhat it does
RemindersView personal reminders.
ProfileView and edit your user profile.
Change passwordUpdate your login credentials.
ThemeSwitch between light and dark display themes.
LogoutSign out of Mareto.

For example, clicking Reminders opens the reminders panel, where you can view existing reminders or click + Add a reminder to create a new one.

Reminders flow — account menu, reminders panel, and create a reminder

Common Sidebar Pages

The pages below appear across most Mareto configurations. Your sidebar may include all, some, or different versions of these depending on your organization’s setup.

Home

Your personalized dashboard. Shows today’s overview — active intakes, open cases, incidents, room checks, bed checks, shift logs, daily logs, and appointments at a glance. Also displays Special Announcements. Home is always your starting point.

Person Registry

A searchable registry of all people in your system. Used to look up existing records, create new person profiles, and avoid creating duplicate entries. Most workflows begin here.

Intake / Central Intake

The log for recording and tracking client intake. Use + New Client to begin a new intake record.

Case / Case Management

Displays your active and closed caseload. Use the Active Cases and Closed Cases tabs to toggle views. Use + New Case to open a new case.

Event Log

A record of events and activities logged against cases and people.

Programs

Manages program enrollment and participation. Expandable — click to reveal sub-pages.

Shelter

Displays current occupancy — occupied beds, available beds, occupancy rate, and average length of stay. Auto-populated; no manual entry required. Expands to reveal Shelter Operations (Bed Check, Room Check, Shift Log, Incident Reports).

Outreach

Records of outreach events including items distributed, emergency services needed, quantities provided, and total participants.

Referrals

Track and manage referrals across programs. Filter by referral status, program context, and type. Use + Add to create a new referral.

Daily Log

A running log of day-to-day notes and observations, including staff @mentions for follow-up.

Appointment

Manages client appointments. List view plus full calendar. Use + Add to book an appointment.

Calendar

Organization-wide calendar for scheduling and viewing events across programs.

Reporting

Aggregate reporting across your organization’s data.

Strategic Insights Dashboard

In-depth analytical view. Read-only — no manual data entry required.

Survey Dashboard

Satisfaction surveys and results. Expands to show Survey Participants, Survey Configuration, and Survey Dispatch.

Tip:

Mareto HQ — Help Centre is always pinned at the bottom of the sidebar. Click it at any time to access guides, video tutorials, support tickets, and announcements from HelpSeeker — without leaving Mareto.

Quick Reference: Where to Find Common Tasks

TaskWhere to go
See today’s activity summaryHome
Look up or create a person recordPerson Registry
Record a new client intakeIntake (or Central Intake) → + New Client
View or open a caseCase (or Case Management)
Check bed and room availabilityShelter
Log a shelter incidentShelter → Shelter Operations → Incident Reports
Record a shift handoverShelter → Shelter Operations → Shift Log
Schedule a client appointmentAppointment → + Add
Write a day-to-day noteDaily Log
View program-level reportsReporting
Access help and guidesMareto HQ (bottom of sidebar)
Change your password or themeYour initials → top-right corner

For additional support, open Mareto HQ at the bottom of the sidebar or submit a support ticket.
